# Issue Proposals

This directory contains structured issue proposals for features and improvements to this project.
Each file maps directly to a GitHub Issue that can be created by a maintainer.

## Motivation

These proposals were generated after reviewing [GitHub's accessibility agents guide](https://accessibility.github.com/documentation/guide/getting-started-with-agents/)
and identifying ways this project could uniquely benefit from GitHub Copilot coding agent capabilities.

## Open Proposals

| # | File | Title | Labels |
|---|------|-------|--------|
| 1 | [001-copilot-agent-accessibility-md-bootstrap.md](./001-copilot-agent-accessibility-md-bootstrap.md) | Add example: GitHub Copilot coding agent to bootstrap ACCESSIBILITY.md for new projects | enhancement, ai-agent, documentation |
| 2 | [002-copilot-agent-remediation-workflow.md](./002-copilot-agent-remediation-workflow.md) | Add example: GitHub Copilot agent workflow for automated accessibility issue remediation | enhancement, ai-agent, ci-cd |
| 3 | [003-agents-md-copilot-agent-mode-guide.md](./003-agents-md-copilot-agent-mode-guide.md) | Add guide: Structuring AGENTS.md for GitHub Copilot agent mode (WCAG-grounded code generation) | enhancement, ai-agent, documentation |

## How to Use These Proposals

To convert a proposal into a GitHub Issue:
1. Open the proposal file and read the full content.
2. Go to [Issues → New Issue](https://github.com/mgifford/ACCESSIBILITY.md/issues/new).
3. Use the title from the proposal's frontmatter `title:` field.
4. Paste the body of the proposal (below the frontmatter) into the issue description.
5. Apply the labels listed in the proposal's `labels:` field.
6. Remove this proposal file once the issue has been created.
